title: CEO Notes
description: Upcoming events, new content from the Naval Institute Press, and a reminder for members to cast their vote in the 2026 Board election.
text:
Save These Dates!
Upcoming Maritime Security Dialogues:
• Chief of Naval Operations Admiral Daryl Caudle—31 March at the Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS).
• Admiral William Houston, U.S. Navy, Director, Naval Nuclear Propulsion Program—22 April at CSIS.
Naval Institute Annual Meeting:
• 13 May at the Jack C. Taylor Conference Center in Annapolis. Additional details coming soon. Visit usni.org/events for more information.
New From the Naval Institute Press
With tensions rising in the Pacific, the prospect of conflict between China and Taiwan and the United States is no longer theoretical. War Plan Taiwan by Rowan Allport explores the evolution of U.S. military planning for such a scenario. Drawing on history, strategy, and technology, it examines OPLAN 5077 and the wider Indo-Pacific defense landscape.
Membership
Special thanks to all who generously supported the Naval Institute Sponsored Student Program for the 2025–2026 academic year. A record number of midshipmen and cadets accepted the gift: at the U.S. Naval Academy, 478 midshipmen; at the U.S. Coast Guard Academy, 168 cadets; and across numerous NROTC battalions, 581 midshipmen. Introducing the benefits of Naval Institute membership to tomorrow’s leaders contributes immeasurably to their professional growth and development. Your gift brings knowledge, history, and insight to eager young professionals, and it is truly appreciated!
Last Chance To Vote
